name: Chocolate-Hazelnut Thumbprints
recipeIngredient: 1 cup + 2 tablespoons Unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the hazelnuts in a small baking pan in a preheated 350°F (175°C) oven for about 7 to 10 minutes, or until the skins start to crack. Cool slightly and place into a kitchen towel; rub together to remove the skins. Allow to cool completely. Process in a food processor until finely ground. Set aside.
2. Sift together the flour, cocoa powder, and salt into a bowl. In a large bowl, beat the butter and sugar with an electric mixer until fluffy. Beat in the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla extract until well combined. Gradually add the flour mixture in three additions, mixing well after each addition. Fold in the ground hazelnuts until evenly distributed.
3. Form the dough into 1-inch balls and place on two ungreased baking sheets. Make a small indentation in the center of each cookie with your thumb. Spoon a little raspberry preserves into each indentation (about 1/4 teaspoon). Bake in a preheated 350°F (175°C) oven for 12 minutes. Transfer to wire racks to cool.
4. Just before serving, lightly dust the cookies with powdered sugar.
